source_refs.html: /Users/toy/English/躺着学/躺着学阅读/8188_Passage 3 Theory or Practice.html

answer_key.q1: B. Business education is not doing their job well.
answer_key.q2: D. Students are ill-prepared for their career from universities.

passages.title: Theory or Practice?
passages.source_html:
Theory or Practice?


What is the point of research carried out by biz schools?


A 


Students go to universities and other academic institutions to prepare for their future. We pay tuition and struggle through classes in the hopes that we can find a fulfilling and exciting career. But the choice of your university has a large influence on your future. How can you know which university will prepare you the best for your future? Like other academic institutions, business schools are judged by the quality of the research carried out by their faculties. Professors must both teach students and also produce original research in their own field. The quality of this research is assessed by academic publications. At the same time, universities have another responsibility to equip their students for the real world, however that is defined. Most students learning from professors will not go into academics themselves – so how do academics best prepare them for their future careers,whatever that may be? Whether academic research actually produces anything that is useful to the practice of business, or even whether it is its job to do so, are questions that can provoke vigorous arguments on campus. 


B 


The debate, which first flared during the 1950s, was reignited in August, when AACSB International, the most widely recognised global accrediting agency for business schools, announced it would consider changing the way it evaluates research. The news followed rather damning criticism in 2002 from Jeffrey Pfeffer, a Stanford professor, and Christina Fong of Washington University, which questioned whether business education in its current guise was sustainable. The study found that traditional modes of academia were not adequately preparing students for the kind of careers they faced in current times. The most controversial recommendation in AACSB’s draft report (which was sent round to administrators for their comment) is that the schools should be required to demonstrate the value of their faculties’ research not simply by listing its citations in journals, but by demonstrating, the impact it has in the professional world. New qualifiers, such as average incomes, student placement in top firms and business collaborations would now be considered just as important as academic publications. 


C 


AACSB justifies its stance by saying that it wants schools and faculty to play to their strengths, whether they be in pedagogy, in the research of practical applications, or in scholarly endeavor. Traditionally, universities operate in a pyramid structure. Everyone enters and stays in an attempt to be successful in their academic field. A psychology professor must publish competitive research in the top neuroscience journals. A Cultural Studies professor must send graduate students on new field research expeditions to be taken seriously. This research is the core of a university’s output. And research of any kind is expensive – AACSB points out that business schools in America alone spend more than $320m a year on it. So it seems legitimate to ask for what purpose it is undertaken. 


D 


If a school chose to specialise in professional outputs rather than academic outputs, it could use such a large sum of money and redirect it into more fruitful programs. For example, if a business school wanted a larger presence of employees at top financial firms, this money maybe better spent on a center which focuses on building the skills of students, rather than paying for more high-level research to be done through the effort of faculty. A change in evaluation could also open the door to inviting more professionals from different fields to teach as adjuncts. Students could take accredited courses from people who are currently working in their dream field. The AACSB insists that universities answer the question as to why research is the most critical component of traditional education. 


E 


On one level, the question is simple to answer. Research in business schools, as anywhere else, is about expanding the boundaries of knowledge; it thrives on answering unasked questions. Surely this pursuit of knowledge is still important to the university system. Our society progresses because we learn how to do things in new ways, a process which depends heavily on research and academics. But one cannot ignore the other obvious practical uses of research publications, Research is also about cementing schools’ – and professors’ – reputations. Schools gain kudos from their faculties’ record of publication: which journals publish them, and how often. In some cases, such as with government-funded schools in Britain, it can affect how much money they receive. For professors,the mantra is often ‘publish or perish’. Their careers depend on being right journals. 


F 


But at a certain point, one has to wonder whether this research is being done for the benefit of the university or for the students the university aims to teach. Greater publications will attract greater funding, which will in turn be spent on better publications. Students seeking to enter professions out of academia find this cycle frustrating, and often see their professors as being part of the ‘Ivory Tower’ of academia, operating in a self-contained community that has little influence on the outside world.


G 


The research is almost universally unread by real-world managers. Part of the trouble is that the journals labour under a similar ethos. They publish more than 20,000 articles each year. Most of the research is highly quantitative, hypothesis-driven and esoteric. As a result, it is almost universally unread by real-world managers. Much of the research criticises other published research. A paper in a 2006 issue of Strategy & Leadership commented that ‘research is not designed with managers’ needs in mind, nor is it communicated in the journals they read. For the most part it has become a self-referential closed system irrelevant to corporate performance.’ The AACSB demands that this segregation must change for the future of higher education. If students must invest thousands of dollars for an education as part of their career path, the academics which serve the students should be more fully incorporated into the professional world. This means that universities must focus on other strengths outside of research, such as professional networks,technology kills, and connections with top business firms around the world. Though many universities resisted the report,today’s world continues to change. The universities which prepare students for our changing future have little choice but to change with new trends and new standards.

groups.title: Questions 27-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 27-29


Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D. Write the correct letter in boxes 27-29 on your answer sheet.


27

In the second paragraph, the recommendation given by AACSB is

正确答案： C. to evaluate how the paper influences the field.

原文答案句：The most controversial recommendation in AACSB’s draft report (which was sent round to administrators for their comment) is that the schools should be required to demonstrate the value of their faculties’ research not simply by listing its citations in journals, but by demonstrating, the impact it has in the professional world.  

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目问的是在第二段中，AACSB提出的建议是什么。  

定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"AACSB"和"recommendation"可以定位到B段中关于AACSB建议的句子。  

解题思路：原文明确提到AACSB的建议是学校需要展示其研究人员的价值，不仅仅是通过列出期刊中的引用，而是通过展示其对专业领域的影响。这与选项C的描述完全一致，即评估论文对领域的影响。选项A与原文的"not simply by listing its citations"相反；选项B未提及；选项D与原文的"changing the way it evaluates research"矛盾。因此，正确答案是C。


28

Why does AACSB put forward the recommendation?

正确答案： A. To give full play to the faculties’ advantage.

原文答案句：AACSB justifies its stance by saying that it wants schools and faculty to play to their strengths, whether they be in pedagogy, in the research of practical applications, or in scholarly endeavor.  

对应段落：C段  

审题：题目问AACSB提出建议的原因是什么。  

定位关键信息：通过题目中的" AACSB"和"recommendation"定位到C段首句，该句明确提到AACSB的立场和动机。  

解题思路：原文中明确指出AACSB希望通过新的评估方式，让学校和教师发挥各自的优势，无论是教学、实践研究还是学术研究。这与选项A" To give full play to the faculties’ advantage"完全一致。其他选项B、C、D的内容在原文中未被提及或与原文表述不符。


29

Why does the author mention the Journal Strategy & Leadership?

正确答案： A. To characterize research as irrelevant to company performance.

原文答案句：A paper in a 2006 issue of Strategy & Leadership commented that ‘research is not designed with managers’ needs in mind, nor is it communicated in the journals they read. For the most part it has become a self-referential closed system irrelevant to corporate performance.’

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问作者提及《战略与领导力》杂志是为了什么。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"Journal Strategy & Leadership"定位到G段中提到该期刊的句子。

解题思路：根据原文中的句子可以看出，《战略与领导力》杂志发表的一篇文章评论道，研究并非以管理人员的需求为出发点，研究论文也不会出现在他们阅读的杂志中，研究大多已演变为自我参照的封闭体系，与公司运营表现毫无关联。因此，对应选项A。选项B提到经理人不读研究论文，虽然原文提到研究几乎不被现实世界的管理人员阅读，但这是研究的普遍情况，而不是提及该期刊的目的。选项C和D的内容在原文中均未提及。


Questions 30-31

 

Choose TWO correct answers



Options (Word Bank):
A. Students should pay less to attend universities.
B. Business education is not doing their job well.
C. Professors should not focus on writing papers.
D. Students are ill-prepared for their career from universities.
E. Recognized accrediting agency can evaluate research well.
30

Which TWO choices are in line with Jeffrey Pfeffer and Christina Fong’s idea?

正确答案： 1 B. Business education is not doing their job well.  |  2 D. Students are ill-prepared for their career from universities.

原文答案句：The news followed rather damning criticism in 2002 from Jeffrey Pfeffer, a Stanford professor, and Christina Fong of Washington University, which questioned whether business education in its current guise was sustainable. The study found that traditional modes of academia were not adequately preparing students for the kind of careers they faced in current times. 

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是Jeffrey Pfeffer和Christina Fong的观点，需要找出与他们的观点相符的两个选项。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的名字"Jeffrey Pfeffer"和"Christina Fong"定位到B段第一句，找到他们发表的研究和观点。

解题思路：原文提到两位教授认为当前的商务教育很难可持续发展，传统的学究式模式不足以帮学生准备好应对职业生涯中的问题。五个选项中，B项说商务教育进展得不够好，D项说大学毕业生没有为职业发展准备好，两个选项的描述与原文意思基本一致。因此，本题的答案为BD。


Questions 32-36


Do the following statements agree with the information given in the passage? In boxes 32-36 on your answer sheet, write TRUE if the statement is true, FALSE if the statement is false, NOT GIVEN if the information is not givern in the passage.


32

The debate about the usefulness of academic research for business practices is a recent one.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：The debate, which first flared during the 1950s, was reignited in August, when AACSB International, the most widely recognised global accrediting agency for business schools, announced it would consider changing the way it evaluates research.  

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目问“关于学术研究对商业实践是否有用的辩论是最近发生的”是否正确。  

定位关键信息：利用题干中的关键词"debate"和"usefulness of academic research for business practices"定位到B段第一句，明确辩论起源于20世纪50年代。  

解题思路：原文明确指出辩论最早在20世纪50年代爆发，并在2002年及之后再次被提及，因此该辩论并非最近才出现的，而是长期存在的。题干说“是最近发生的”与原文信息矛盾，故答案为False。


33

AACSB's draft report was not reviewed externally.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：The most controversial recommendation in AACSB’s draft report (which was sent round to administrators for their comment) is that the schools should be required to demonstrate the value of their faculties’ research not simply by listing its citations in journals, but by demonstrating, the impact it has in the professional world.  

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目问国际高等商学院协会（AACSB）的报告草案是否没有经过外部审读。  

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"AACSB’s draft report"和"reviewed externally"，在文章中定位到B段中提到AACSB的报告草案送去给administrators（管理人员）征求意见。  

解题思路：原文第2段“The most controversial recommendation in AACSB's draft report（which was sent round to administrators for their comment）.”通过括号中的内容可以看出，该草案已分发给管理人员征求意见，经过了外部审查。因此，本题的答案为False。


34

Business schools in the US spend more than 320 million dollars yearly on research.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：And research of any kind is expensive – AACSB points out that business schools in America alone spend more than $320m a year on it.  

对应段落：C段  

审题：题目问的是“美国的商学院每年在研究上花费超过3.2亿美元”这一陈述是否与文章信息一致。  

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是“Business schools in the US”（美国的商学院）、“spend more than 320 million dollars yearly”（每年花费超过3.2亿美元）和“on research”（在研究上）。这些关键词可以帮助我们在文章中定位相关信息。  

解题思路：在C段中，原文明确提到“AACSB points out that business schools in America alone spend more than $320m a year on it”，其中“it”指代的是前文提到的“research”。这句话直接表明美国的商学院每年在研究上花费超过3.2亿美元，与题目陈述完全一致。因此，答案为A（True）。


35

Many universities pursue professional outputs.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：If a school chose to specialise in professional outputs rather than academic outputs, it could use such a large sum of money and redirect it into more fruitful programs. 

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是“许多大学追求专业产出”是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：使用 "universities", "pursue", "professional outputs" 作为关键词，定位文中描述大学实际目标或选择的句子。

解题思路：段落D中，原文说的是“If a school chose...”（如果一所学校选择...），这表示一种假设的情况，而非对现实状况的描述。文章通篇都在讨论AACSB提出的改革建议以及关于研究价值的辩论，但没有提供任何信息表明已经有“许多大学”将工作重心转向了追求专业产出。题目信息在原文中找不到依据，因此答案为NOT GIVEN。

36

Greater publications benefit professors and students as well.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：Greater publications will attract greater funding, which will in turn be spent on better publications. Students seeking to enter professions out of academia find this cycle frustrating, and often see their professors as being part of the ‘Ivory Tower’ of academia, operating in a self-contained community that has little influence on the outside world.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是“更优秀的论文发表对教授和学生都是有好处的”，是否与文章一致。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词“Greater publications”和“benefit professors and students”可以帮助我们在文章中定位答案。

解题思路：根据E段的内容，我们可以确定更多的出版物对教授是有利的，因为它们可以巩固学校的声誉，对教授的职业生涯也有帮助。然而，F段中提到，对于不从事研究的学生来说，教授们的研究使他们感到“frustrating”，并认为教授们脱离了现实世界。这表明更多的发表并未给这部分学生带来好处。所以，答案是B（False）。


Questions 37-40


Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A-E, below. Write the correct letter, A-E, in boxes 37-40 on your answer sheet.


A it progresses as we learn innovative ways of doing things. 

B the trends and standards are changing. 

C their jobs depend on it. 

D they care about their school rankings and government funds. 

E it helps students to go into top business firms. 


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
37

37 Most professors support academic research because
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：Research is also about cementing schools’ – and professors’ – reputations. Schools gain kudos from their faculties’ record of publication: which journals publish them, and how often. In some cases, such as with government-funded schools in Britain, it can affect how much money they receive. For professors,the mantra is often ‘publish or perish’. Their careers depend on being right journals.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是“大多数教授支持学术研究的原因是什么？”

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词“Most professors support academic research”，可以在E段找到对应的句子。

解题思路：根据原文可以确定，教授们支持学术研究是因为他们可以从中收获名誉，他们的职涯也依赖于发表研究成果。这与选项C“their jobs depend on it”相符合。
38

38 schools support academic research because
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：Research is also about cementing schools’ – and professors’ – reputations. Schools gain kudos from their faculties’ record of publication: which journals publish them, and how often. In some cases, such as with government-funded schools in Britain, it can affect how much money they receive.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问学校支持学术研究的原因是什么。

定位关键信息：通过题目的"schools support academic research"定位到E段，该段讨论了学术研究对学校和教授的好处。

解题思路：原文明确提到"Research is also about cementing schools’ – and professors’ – reputations"（研究也是为了巩固学校和教授的声誉），并具体解释了学校通过学术论文获得声誉和资金。因此，学校支持学术研究的原因是为了提升声誉和获得更多资金，选择D。
39

39 our society needs academic research because
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：Our society progresses because we learn how to do things in new ways, a process which depends heavily on research and academics.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是“我们的社会需要学术研究是因为什么”，需要根据文章内容找出社会需要学术研究的原因。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的“our society needs academic research because”可以定位到E段，该段讨论了社会需要学术研究的原因。

解题思路：根据E段中的原文答案句“our society progresses because we learn how to do things in new ways, a process which depends heavily on research and academics.”可以得出，社会之所以需要学术研究，是因为社会之所以进步，源于我们不断推陈出新，而这又极大地依赖于学术研究。因此，答案为A。
40

40 universities resisting the AACSB should change because
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：Though many universities resisted the report,today’s world continues to change. The universities which prepare students for our changing future have little choice but to change with new trends and new standards.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问的是为什么抵制AACSB的大学应该改变。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"universities resisting the AACSB"和"change"可以定位到G段最后一句。

解题思路：G段最后一句提到，尽管许多大学对该报告持反对态度，但当今世界仍在不断变化。如果要为学生应对未来变革做准备，大学别无选择，只能顺应新趋势、新标准而改变。因此，原因是世界在变化，大学需要顺应新的趋势和标准，选择B。
